subject: Physics
title: Coulomb's Law of Charges
description: One-minute skill: Coulomb's Law of Charges.

hook.text: Electric charges attract or repel each other with a force that follows a remarkably simple mathematical law.

buildup.text: Charles-Augustin de Coulomb measured this force in 1785 using a sensitive torsion balance.

discovery.text: The force between charges is proportional to both charges and inversely proportional to the square of the distance.

twist.text: Coulomb's law looks almost identical to Newton's gravity law. Two fundamental forces sharing one mathematical shape.

climax.text: But the electric force is 10^36 times stronger than gravity. It dominates at atomic scales.

punchline.text: Like charges repel. Opposites attract. The math mirrors gravity.

quiz.question: How does the electric force compare to gravity in strength?
options: Gravity is stronger at all distances
